header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Meraki MS130 and MS150 Series switches (native Meraki cloud-managed access switches — distinct from the Catalyst 9200L-M/9300L-M hybrid Meraki-managed models, which live in their own C9200/C9300 KB files). Carries the catalog (atomic SKU facts), groups (named shared compatibility sets), and switch models for MS130 (compact access) and MS150 (stackable access). MS130 (11 models: 8/8P/8P-I/8X/12X/24/24P/24X/48/48P/48X) and MS150 (12 models: 24T-4G/24P-4G/24T-4X/24P-4X/24MP-4X/48T-4G/48LP-4G/48FP-4G/48T-4X/48LP-4X/48FP-4X/48MP-4X) are both fully complete: licensing catalog/groups, stacking cables (MS150 only), and switch models. Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco Meraki MS130 Series Data Sheet
Cisco Meraki MS150 Series Data Sheet

design_notes: Pass 1: MS130 licensing — catalog.licenses + groups.license_groups, from MS130 datasheet excerpts pasted into the authoring session.

Pass 2 (this pass): 11 MS130 switch models added (8/8P/8P-I/8X/12X/24/24P/24X/48/48P/48X), from the two MS130 spec-table images/text pasted into the authoring session. No network_modules (fixed uplinks only, uplink_modular=false throughout) and no stack_cables/stackpower_cables (no stacking on this series, confirmed below). Four modeling decisions were confirmed with the user (AskUserQuestion) before authoring: (1) PoE type — every PoE model states 'PoE Type: 802.3bt' with a 30W per-port budget; mapped to registry poe_type 'poe+' since its level_watts (30W) matches exactly, rather than inventing an '802.3bt' enum value. (2) -X model ports — mGbe RJ45 ports (8X/12X/24X/48X) modeled as role=access, speeds=[1g,2.5g] (100M omitted, sub-1G isn't a tracked speed); 10GbE SFP+ ports modeled as role=uplink, speeds=[1g,10g] (dual-rate, matching how other SFP+ uplinks are modeled elsewhere in this project). (3) Stacking — no stacking row appears anywhere in the pasted MS130 tables; confirmed as stacking_capable=false/stackpower_capable=false for all 11 models, with no PSU redundancy either (single fixed PSU only, no secondary/redundant option on any model). (4) PSU wattage — no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage is given anywhere in the tables (only 'Power Input' voltage x current, and 'Power Load idle/max'); confirmed approach is to use each model's own Power Load (max) figure as a conservative proxy for the SYNTHETIC catalog PSU's watts field (same synthetic-placeholder convention as C9200CX's fixed/internal supplies), flagged _incomplete:['power_supply_watts'] on every model and tracked in THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md pending a real nameplate rating.

The 24/48-series models' 'Dedicated Mgmt Interface' row is recorded as attributes.dedicated_mgmt_interface (boolean) rather than a ports[] row, since the registry's port_role enum only has 'access'/'uplink' — an out-of-band management port isn't a registered role, so per this project's own axis/variable/attribute trichotomy it is a display-only attribute, excluded from total_port_count.

'MS130' and 'MS150' were added to the 'series' enum in switching-axes.json with explicit human sign-off (structural change, per this schema's $authoring.cheap_vs_structural). No registry_version bump was made for this addition since no axis KIND/ROLE/shape changed — only its legal_values grew, matching how registry_version is scoped in this project (a bump is for a structural/typed change to the axis SET, not every enum-value addition).

MS130 classic license structure (regime meraki-classic, shape term-based): the datasheet's classic table prints the LIC-MS130-CMPT(A)/-24(A)/-48(A) base order codes each against ONE representative model row, but the 'compact switch (8/12)' / '24 ports' / '48 ports' wording in the Description column states each SKU applies to the WHOLE port-class family, not just the row it's printed on. Confirmed with the user: 'compact' = MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X (both Enterprise and Advanced SKUs available across all five); '24' = MS130-24/-24P/-24X; '48' = MS130-48/-48P/-48X. Per-term SKU strings (-1Y/-3Y/.../-10Y) are synthesized by appending suffixes to the datasheet's base order code (datasheet prints only the base code + a stated term list), mirroring the C9200L-M convention. Enterprise offers 1/3/5/7/10-year terms; Advanced offers 1/3/5-year terms only (no 7/10y) — confirmed uniform across all three port classes.

MS130 subscription license structure (regime meraki-subscription, shape subscription-based, term_years null): LIC-MS-100-{S,M,L}-{E,A}, tier-split per the C9200L-M LIC-MS-200-{M,L}-{E,A} convention. MS100 Small = compact class (8/12-port models); MS100 Medium = 24-port class; MS100 Large = 48-port class.

Tier vocabulary: MS130's own tier names are Enterprise/Advanced; mapped to the registry's existing essentials/advantage legal_values (Enterprise->essentials, Advanced->advantage), same mapping convention used for C9200L-M. No new license_tier axis value.

port_class on the compact-class license catalog entries and groups is recorded as null (not 8), since that class spans two distinct port counts (8-port and 12-port models) and the schema's port_class field is a single integer-or-null.
A trailing 'MS120' line at the end of the user's pasted subscription table was confirmed to be a paste artifact (not MS130 data) and is not reflected anywhere in this file.

Pass 3 (this pass): MS150 licensing (catalog.licenses + license_groups) and stack_cables/stack_cable_groups, from MS150 datasheet excerpts pasted into the authoring session. MS150 model entries are deferred to Pass 4. Same 'compact (8/12)'-style bundling pattern as MS130's classic table: the datasheet prints LIC-MS150-24(A)/-48(A) each against one representative model row, but the '24 ports'/'48 ports' wording covers the whole port-class family. Confirmed with the user: 24-port class = MS150-24T-4G/-24P-4G/-24T-4X/-24P-4X/-24MP-4X; 48-port class = MS150-48T-4G/-48LP-4G/-48FP-4G/-48T-4X/-48LP-4X/-48FP-4X/-48MP-4X. Unlike MS130, this MS150 excerpt does not itself restate a term-year list for the classic SKUs; per confirmed user direction, the MS130 term structure (Enterprise 1/3/5/7/10y, Advanced 1/3/5y) was reused as-is rather than left unsourced.

MS150 subscription licensing reuses the EXACT SAME catalog SKUs already added for MS130 (LIC-MS-100-M-E/-A for the 24-port/'MS100 Medium' class, LIC-MS-100-L-E/-A for the 48-port/'MS100 Large' class) — the MS150 datasheet prints the identical base order codes for its own port classes, confirming these are shared cross-family SKUs. No new subscription catalog.licenses entries were added for MS150; the 4 existing entries' notes were updated to record the added MS150 coverage, and 4 new MS150-specific license_groups (ms150-sub-*) reference those same catalog ids with MS150's own applies_to model list.

MS150 stacking cables: the datasheet's Stacking Cables table visually groups MA-CBL-100G-50CM/-1M/-3M against different model subsets (by port count and -4G vs -4X suffix), mirroring the same blank-cell/merged-cell layout seen in the license tables. Confirmed with the user that, unlike the license tables, this is NOT a per-model compatibility restriction: all three cable lengths are usable across all 12 MS150 models. Modeled as ONE shared stack_cable_group ('ms150-stack-cables') referenced by every MS150 model, rather than per-port-class subsets.

Pass 4 (this pass): 12 MS150 switch models added (24T-4G/24P-4G/24T-4X/24P-4X/24MP-4X/48T-4G/48LP-4G/48FP-4G/48T-4X/48LP-4X/48FP-4X/48MP-4X), from the two MS150 spec-table images/text pasted into the authoring session. No network_modules (fixed uplinks only). Two modeling decisions were confirmed with the user (AskUserQuestion) before authoring: (1) Stacking technology — every model states 'Stacking Ports: 2' / 'Stacking Bandwidth: 80Gbps', but none of the registry's existing stacking_technology legal_values (all Cisco StackWise) fit Meraki's own mechanism; 'meraki-stack-100g' was added to switching-axes.json with explicit human sign-off (structural change; no registry_version bump, same convention as the 'series' addition). All 12 MS150 models carry stacking_capable=true, stacking_technology='meraki-stack-100g', stackpower_capable=false (no StackPower concept in the datasheet). (2) Mixed PoE on the -MP-4X models — 24MP-4X/48MP-4X each have TWO different PoE ceilings in one chassis (standard 1G ports vs the 8/16 mGig-capable ports, which the datasheet's own footnote says reach 'PoE++ up to 60W'); since the registry's poe_type is a single scalar per model with no per-port-group level, poe_type was set to 'upoe' (registry level_watts 60W matches the mGig-port ceiling) per confirmed user direction — 'the switches barely provide enough power to do 60W on those ports anyway'. The per-port asymmetry (which ports reach the higher rate) is recorded in axis_value_provenance and attributes.poe_port_budget_note, since the axis system has no way to express it structurally. poe_budget_watts (the total switch-wide budget, 370W/740W) is unambiguous either way. PSU wattage proxy reuses the exact approach confirmed for MS130 (each model's own Power Load-max, here rounded to the nearest integer since several MS150 Power Load figures are non-integer, e.g. 32.4W/422.2W — flagged _incomplete:['power_supply_watts'] on every model, same as MS130.

mGbe RJ45 ports on the -MP-4X models are modeled as role=access, speeds=[1g,2.5g,5g] (the datasheet states these ports top out at 5G, one level higher than MS130's 2.5G mGbe ports — 5g is already a legal port_speed value in the registry, no axis change needed). 10GbE SFP+ uplinks reuse the same [1g,10g] dual-rate convention established for MS130's -X models. 'Layer 3 Routing: Static Routing' and 'Stacking Ports'/'Stacking Bandwidth' are recorded as plain display attributes (attributes.layer3_routing, attributes.stacking_ports, attributes.stacking_bandwidth_gbps) rather than new axes, since every MS150 model has the identical value (no discriminating/filtering need identified yet).

axis_value_provenance.ports: MS150 Datasheet spec table: access = 48x copper [1g] (RJ45), uplink = 4x fiber [1g/10g] (SFP+). total_port_count = access sum = 48.

axis_value_provenance.stacking: MS150 Datasheet: 'Stacking Ports: 2', 'Stacking Bandwidth: 80Gbps' on every model. stacking_technology='meraki-stack-100g' added to the registry with explicit human sign-off (not a Cisco StackWise technology). stackpower_capable=false (no StackPower row/concept in the datasheet).

models.description: Cisco Meraki MS150-48MP-4X, native cloud-managed access switch. 48-port class, PoE (802.3bt, up to 60W on mGig ports), fixed internal power, fixed internal fan. Stacking-capable (2x stacking ports, 80Gbps).

axis_value_provenance.ports: MS150 Datasheet spec table: access = 32x copper [1g] + 16x copper [1g/2.5g/5g] (RJ45/mGbe RJ45), uplink = 4x fiber [1g/10g] (SFP+). total_port_count = access sum = 48.
axis_value_provenance.poe_*: MS150 Datasheet: 'PoE Type: 802.3bt', 'PoE Port Budget: 60W**' (footnote: PoE++ up to 60W on the mGig-capable ports only), 'PoE Switch Budget: 740W'. poe_type mapped to registry 'upoe' (level_watts 60W matches the stated mGig-port ceiling); this is a MIXED-PoE chassis (standard ports vs mGig ports have different ceilings, not separately quantified in the datasheet) — 'upoe' records the switch's maximum deliverable capability, per confirmed user direction. poe_budget_watts = stated PoE Switch Budget (unambiguous, applies switch-wide regardless of the per-port asymmetry).
axis_value_provenance.stacking: MS150 Datasheet: 'Stacking Ports: 2', 'Stacking Bandwidth: 80Gbps' on every model. stacking_technology='meraki-stack-100g' added to the registry with explicit human sign-off (not a Cisco StackWise technology). stackpower_capable=false (no StackPower row/concept in the datasheet).
axis_value_provenance.power_supply_watts: SYNTHETIC placeholder (MS150-48MP-4X-PSU-SYN): no PSU part number or nameplate wattage is given in the datasheet. watts = this model's own stated Power Load (max) = 933W, rounded to the nearest integer (933) for the schema's integer-typed catalog field, used as a conservative proxy per the same approach confirmed for MS130 (flagged _incomplete; see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md).
